$comment: B0 — the deciding control set for 'does per-piece tagging pay for itself'. Two arms on ONE axis. BENEFIT: the correct answer requires splitting INSIDE a whitespace word, which a word-unit tagger cannot represent at any confidence — its unit of decision is the whole word, so at best it assigns the word one tag and is wrong on the other component. COST: multi-piece digit runs where our per-piece emissions can disagree with themselves. Every row states its expected components so neither parser is graded against an opinion. Sources for the expectations are cited per row; where a convention is genuinely contested the row is marked contested=true and excluded from the verdict count.

benefit.why: AU/NZ unit-slash-number. ONE whitespace word `12/345` carries TWO components.
Queens hyphenated house number is ONE component `123-45`, not two. The inverse test: a word-unit tagger gets this FREE, we must not split it.
Wisconsin grid address — ONE house_number token with embedded directionals. Inverse test again: must NOT split.
postcode + cedex marker; cedex is a distinct component in our schema
glued DE street+number, no space. ONE word, TWO components.
benefit.source: DE; glue is a real OCR/user-entry artifact

benefit.why: glued unit designator+id. ONE word `Apt4B` -> unit.
benefit.$note: contested: whether `Apt4B` is one unit span or unit-designator+id is a schema call, not a parser call. Excluded from the count.

benefit.why: JP block-lot-number: ONE word carrying the whole address-number structure
benefit.$note: contested: our schema has no JP block/lot decomposition; excluded.

cost.why: MEASURED incoherence: B-house_number 0.604 on `4`, I-postcode 0.587/0.765 on `4`,`B`
MEASURED incoherence: B-house_number 0.313 on `3`, I-postcode 0.657/0.643 on `9`,`A`
3-digit run, street segments CORRECTLY then the digit goes to postcode

cost.$note: contested: whether a `100 104` range is one house_number span or two is a schema call. Excluded.

cost.why: THE CONTROL for the cost arm: same street stem, one word shorter, and it reads house_number CORRECTLY. If the cost arm is really about intra-word incoherence, this row should behave like its 3-word sibling. It does not.
THE FAMILIARITY CONTROL: identical digit run + identical shape, FAMILIAR street. Isolates street-familiarity from digit-run length.
